items.title: Anwar launches national Teachers’ Day 2026, praises educators’ role in nation‑building
items.summary: IPOH, May 16 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today officiated the 55th National-Level Teachers’ Day Celebration 2026 held at Hotel Casuarina @ Meru here. Also present at the celebration were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek, Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad, as well as about 3,000 educators from across the country. In her speech, Fadhlina announced 12 exciting initiatives to empower and strengthen teachers’ duties, including the continuation of free Google Certified Educator (GCE) Level One and Level Two certification courses for all teachers.

items.title: Govt raises allocation to RM900mil to build 6,000 new classrooms
items.summary: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the additional funding was necessary following a reassessment by the Education Ministry, which reported an increase in student enrolment and classroom requirements. "However, after reassessment and current projections, the government has agreed to increase the allocation from RM500 million to RM900 million to build 6,000 classrooms," he said. He also assured that Bumiputera small contractors who perform well would be given opportunities to undertake larger government projects in the future.

items.title: Aiman Athirah: 15 teenagers, 1,866 foreigners among 6,680 issued littering notices
items.summary: Deputy Housing and Local Government Minister Datuk Aiman Athirah Sabu said the figure included 1,866 foreigners, among them individuals from Indonesia, Singapore, China and Bangladesh. Also present was Solid Waste and Public Cleansing Management Corporation (SWCorp) chief executive officer Khalid Mohamed. Meanwhile, Aiman Athirah said the teenagers, aged between 16 and 18, most of whom committed the offences in Kuala Lumpur, would undergo the same punishment process, including Community Service Orders.

items.title: Govt to review education ministry allocation, considers increase in upcoming budget, says PM
items.summary: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said budget allocations will be increased in line with the current needs of the national education sector. In addition, Anwar said the Madani Government has implemented various initiatives for teachers’ welfare, including maintenance and upgrading of teachers’ rooms in 3,500 schools. We have carried out maintenance on 7,000 teachers’ rooms,” he said.

items.title: Nicotine delisting helped regulate multibillion-ringgit vape industry, says Dr Zaliha
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former health minister Datuk Dr Zaliha Mustafa has defended her 2023 decision to remove liquid nicotine from the Poisons Act 1952, saying the move paved the way to fill a legal gap and bring the multibillion-ringgit vape industry out of the black market. She said before March 2023, enforcement under the Poisons Act 1952 was insufficient to curb the proliferation of vape products in the market. She said that to address the regulatory gap, the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Bill was tabled in Parliament in June 2023 and has since come into force as the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act 2024 (Act 852).

items.title: DBKL detains three over illegal parking fee collection in Pudu
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Three men were detained by Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) during an enforcement operation against illegal parking fee collectors. The three local men were detained during the operation at Jalan Yew and Jalan Pasar Baharu in Pudu on Thursday. The operation was carried out following public complaints regarding individuals allegedly collecting parking fees without authorisation.

items.title: Govt closely monitoring Malaysia-led Gaza aid flotilla amid fears of interception, says Anwar
items.summary: IPOH, May 16 — Malaysia is closely monitoring the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) humanitarian mission to Gaza after the convoy entered high-risk waters where organisers fear possible interception, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today, He said the government remains on standby as the flotilla, involving 54 ships and nearly 500 activists from 45 countries, approaches areas near Gaza that organisers say carry a high risk of action by Israeli forces. The latest mission comes after an earlier Global Sumud Flotilla attempt reportedly saw several vessels intercepted in international waters, with activists detained and some boats damaged or allegedly sabotaged. SNCC previously said 10 Malaysians involved in the earlier mission were among several participants detained several days by Israeli forces last month, and later released in Turkiye.

items.title: PM: Teacher influencers must uphold ethics online
items.summary: IPOH: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has reminded educators who are active as social media influencers to uphold proper conduct and use their platforms responsibly in shaping public discourse. He said teachers who engage online should set a positive example by presenting facts clearly and expressing differing views with courtesy, good manners and strong cultural values. "They should set an example by explaining facts clearly and using proper manners and good culture when criticising, whether they agree or disagree," he said after presenting letters of acceptance to Bumiputera small contractors at the Perak Education Complex in Meru here today.

items.title: Immigration detains 152 foreigners in Changkat vice raid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department detained 152 foreigners after busting an alleged prostitution syndicate operating behind a three-star hotel in Changkat. The department said the operation, conducted at 1.30am yesterday, was carried out with the Customs Department following public complaints and two weeks of intelligence gathering by its enforcement division at the Putrajaya headquarters. The department said the operation targeted premises spanning the first to sixth floors of the building.

items.title: Govt announces 12 initiatives to ease teachers' workload, improve welfare
items.summary: -A Special Federal Training Award (HLP) for teachers serving in hardship schools under ProKhas, including Hospital Schools, Floating Schools and rural schools in P1, P2 and P3 areas. -A RM169 million allocation to provide devices to every school nationwide. -An additional RM100 million allocation for the maintenance and upgrading of teachers' rooms, bringing the total allocation to RM200 million.

items.title: Govt on standby over safety concerns involving Sumud convoy, says Anwar
items.summary: IPOH: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the government is closely monitoring developments involving the Sumud convoy amid concerns over the group's safety as it enters a critical phase of its mission. He said Putrajaya remained on standby and was maintaining direct communication with the convoy to ensure necessary assistance could be provided if needed. Anwar said Malaysia was prepared to respond should any untoward incident occur involving participants of the humanitarian mission.

items.title: Onn Hafiz says BN will go solo in Johor polls, rules out electoral pact with Pakatan, Perikatan, and Muda
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 16 — Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) today confirmed it will contest all 56 seats in the next state election, effectively ruling out any electoral cooperation with Pakatan Harapan (PH), Perikatan Nasional (PN) or Muda. “With a sense of conviction and responsibility, Johor BN will contest in all 56 state constituencies in the upcoming state election. In the 2022 Johor election, BN won 40 of the 56 state seats, dominated by Umno victories.

items.title: Man arrested after viral wheelie stunt on Jalan Raja Laut
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 19-year-old man was arrested after posting a video of him performing a wheelie and running a red light on Jalan Raja Laut on social media. District police Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said officers detected the viral video yesterday on the suspect's Instagram account, which showed him riding a black Yamaha Y15ZR while performing the dangerous stunt and running a red light. He said the incident was believed to have taken place on April 25 between 2am and 3am on Jalan Raja Laut.

items.title: Psychiatrist, police officer recalled in Zara Qairina inquest next week
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: A consultant forensic psychiatrist and a police officer will be recalled to continue their testimonies at the inquest into the death of Zara Qairina Mahathir next week. Lawyer Shahlan Jufri said consultant forensic psychiatrist Dr Chua Sze Hung of Hospital Mesra Bukit Padang is expected to testify again. Shahlan, whose legal team represents Zara Qairina's mother, Noraidah Lamat, said they would examine the 67th witness on Monday, as they had previously reserved their questions when the expert testified last month.

items.title: 'Rumah Bangsa' a platform to defend Malay 'red lines', says KJ
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Umno's Rumah Bangsa initiative serves as a common platform for various parties to defend "red lines" involving the Malays and Islam, says former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin. "These red lines cover sensitivities related to Islam, the royal institution, Malay special privileges and the Malay language," Khairy said at an event celebrating Umno's 80th anniversary here. Khairy said the Rumah Bangsa initiative did not suggest problems with other races, and that relations between the races remained harmonious.

items.title: Mara to sponsor future pilots, with tahfiz STEM students first in line
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) will soon open applications for a pilot cadet financing programme, with priority to be given to tahfiz students from the STEM stream. Mara chairman Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said advertisements and eligibility requirements for the programme would be announced soon. Asyraf said the minimum requirement for the programme includes students from the science, technology, engineering and mathematics stream.

items.title: Two motorcyclists arrested over dangerous wheelie stunts on SPE
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Two men were arrested after a video shoswing them performing dangerous "wheelie" stunts on high-powered motorcycles along the Setiawangsa-Pantai Expressway (SPE) went viral on social media. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief, Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa, said the incident occurred on May 9 at 11am along the SPE from the city centre heading towards Putrajaya. He said investigations found the two suspects were riding a red Ducati Hypermotard 950 and a black Ducati Panigale.

items.title: Perikatan names Kemaman MP Ahmad Samsuri as new Opposition Leader, signals openness to electoral pact
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — Perikatan Nasional (PN) has appointed coalition chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar as the new Opposition Leader in Parliament. “The council agreed to appoint Kemaman MP Datuk Seri Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar to shoulder the responsibility as Opposition Leader,” PN secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan said in a Facebook statement this afternoon. He also announced PN’s new line-up of state heads ahead of preparations for the next general election, as follows: Perlis — Datuk Seri Shahidan Kassim Kedah — Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor Penang — Datuk Dominic Lau Hoe Chai Perak — Datuk Seri Ahmad Faizal Azumu Selangor — Datuk Seri Mohamed Azmin Ali Federal Territories and Melaka — Datuk Mohd Radzi Md Jidin Johor — Datuk Dr Sahruddin Jamal Pahang — Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man Terengganu — Datuk Alias Razak Kelantan — Datuk Ahmad Yakob Sabah — Datuk Aliakbar Gulasan Sarawak — Jaziri Alkaf Abdillah Suffian The only state without a head for now is Negeri Sembilan, which Takiyudddin listed as being “deferred”.

items.title: Johor BN set to contest all 56 seats in state polls [WATCH]
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) will contest all 56 state seats in the coming election. "Barisan Nasional Johor will contest all 56 state seats in the coming state election," said the menteri besar. The next Johor state election must be held by April next year, unless the state assembly is dissolved earlier.

items.title: Taiwan declares itself independent after Trump's warning
items.summary: TAIPEI: Taiwan said Saturday it is an "independent" nation, hours after US President Donald Trump warned the democratic island against declaring formal independence. Taiwan depends heavily on US security backing to deter China from carrying out its threat to annex the island by force. The ministry also insisted that US arms sales were part of Washington's security commitment to Taiwan, after Trump flagged that he was considering the issue.

items.title: Abdul Rashid dedicates national award to classroom teachers nationwide
items.summary: IPOH: Retired educator Abdul Rashid Bakar has dedicated his Teacher's Day recognition to classroom teachers nationwide, saying the honour reflects the quiet but enduring contributions of those who have spent their careers teaching behind classroom doors. I see this award as representing teachers who are still teaching in classrooms. I am very grateful," Rashid said.

items.title: Palestinian health ministry reports deadly Israeli raid in Jenin camp
items.summary: RAMALLAH: Palestinian health officials said Israeli forces killed a man on Saturday on the outskirts of the Jenin refugee camp in the northern occupied West Bank. The health ministry in Ramallah identified the victim as 34-year-old Nour al-Din Kamal Hassan Fayyad, saying he was "killed by occupation forces' fire in the Jenin camp." The operation, dubbed "Iron Wall", has targeted Jenin and Tulkarem camps and displaced nearly 40,000 Palestinians, according to the UN agency for Palestinian refugees UNRWA.

items.title: Putin to visit China May 19-20, days after Trump trip
items.summary: MOSCOW: Russian President Vladimir Putin will travel to China on May 19 for a two-day visit, hot on the heels of US President Donald Trump's trip to Beijing, the Kremlin said on Saturday. During the trip, the Russian leader will discuss with his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping how to "further strengthen the comprehensive partnership and strategic cooperation" between Moscow and Beijing, according to a Kremlin statement. Before Trump arrived in China, Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy asked the US leader to raise the matter of how to stop the conflict during the talks with Xi.

items.title: Klang hit-and-run leaves two sisters dead and 17-year-old boy injured
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — A family walk to dinner in Bandar Baru Klang, Selangor ended in the death of two women and the hospitalisation of a 17-year-old boy after a hit-and-run along Jalan Pekan Baru at about 7.40pm yesterday. North Klang police chief Assistant Commissioner S. Vijaya Rao said the deceased were two sisters aged 57 and 62 while the survivor was the son of one of the women, The Star reported today. He said investigations so far indicated that a silver SUV travelling from Jalan Meru towards Bandar Baru Klang crashed into the three pedestrians who were on the road shoulder before fleeing the scene.

items.title: Trump sees arms sales to Taiwan as negotiating chip with China
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said on Saturday that his pending decision on further arms sales to the island of Taiwan claimed by China was an effective means of pressuring Beijing, reported German news agency dpa. He said he was leaving the arms deal, worth US$14 billion and already approved by the US Congress, up in the air. The US Congress pre-approved the latest arms deal with Taiwan in January 2025.

items.title: Khairy urges BN to contest Johor polls solo to test strength
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The Johor state election is the best platform to test BN's strength by contesting solo, former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin said. We will soon hear the drumbeat for state elections in the South, and the dissolution of the Johor state assembly depends on the Regent of Johor. "In my view, we should test BN's real strength by contesting solo in the Johor state election.

items.title: Malaysia tightens seabass imports, restricts five shrimp species from Thailand
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Agriculture and Food Security Ministry will tighten import controls on fisheries products from Thailand by enforcing the requirement for a Certificate of Analysis (CoA) for siakap (seabass), as well as imposing a temporary restriction on five shrimp species effective June 1. The ministry said the temporary restriction involves the shrimp species Penaeus esculentes (brown tiger prawn), Fenneropenaeus merguiensis (banana prawn), Penaeus vannamei (whiteleg shrimp), Penaeus monodon (giant tiger prawn) and Penaeus stylirostris (blue shrimp). It added that the measure is aimed at strengthening national food safety controls, particularly to ensure that seabass and shrimp entering the local market comply with established safety and quality standards.

items.title: Woman loses over RM50,000 in part-time job scam
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: A woman lost RM55,334 after falling victim to an online scam involving a non-existent part-time job offer. "The task only required the victim to verify products on a digital platform via links provided by the suspect. The victim completed three initial tasks and received RM1,176 in payments.

items.title: KJ calls for clear explanation on UEC admission to public universities
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The Higher Education Ministry has been urged to provide a clear and continuous explanation regarding the government's decision to allow Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) graduates to enrol in public universities. For tahfiz leavers, the identified programmes include the Diploma and Bachelor of Da'wah, Diploma and Bachelor of Al-Quran and As-Sunnah, Bachelor of Tahfiz Education, and Bachelor of Islamic Studies (Da'wah). Meanwhile, for SMPC/UEC leavers, the programmes include the Bachelor of Arts (Chinese Language Studies) with Honours, Bachelor of Chinese Language and Linguistics, Bachelor of Arts (Chinese Studies), and Bachelor of Chinese Language with Education.

items.title: PM launches Paduri Madani scheme to empower B40 women entrepreneurs with loans up to RM30,000
items.summary: IPOH, May 16 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today launched the PADURI Madani Scheme under Amanah Ikhtiar Malaysia (AIM), aimed at empowering women, particularly those in the B40 group, through entrepreneurship. Who are women? “AIM encourages women to prove their ability to borrow and repay loans.

items.title: MOT unveils aviation stability measures, RM5m rebates expected to benefit 100,000 travellers
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 16 — The Ministry of Transport (MOT), together with the Civil Aviation Authority of Malaysia (CAAM) and Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB), has introduced several targeted intervention measures to ensure the stability of the country’s aviation sector amid geopolitical uncertainties affecting industry operations and costs. In a statement posted on its official Facebook page today, MOT said the measures include financial support, temporary restructuring of operational costs and targeted incentives to ensure the continuity of domestic air travel, particularly for routes linking Peninsular Malaysia with Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan. “These focused intervention measures are aimed at maintaining operational stability and safeguarding consumer interests.

items.title: No info on Jho Low entering Malaysia, says Immigration DG
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Immigration Department has no information regarding allegations that fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low, entered Malaysia with a high-level Chinese delegation last year. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the department had not received any information related to the claim. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said police had yet to verify the claims.

items.title: Malaysia to enforce seabass certificate rule, restrict shrimp imports from Thailand June 1
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 16 — The Ministry of Agriculture and Food Security (MAFS) will tighten import controls on fisheries products from Thailand by enforcing the requirement for a Certificate of Analysis (CoA) for siakap (seabass), as well as imposing a temporary restriction on five shrimp species effective June 1. MAFS said the temporary restriction involves the shrimp species Penaeus esculentes (brown tiger prawn), Fenneropenaeus merguiensis (banana prawn), Penaeus vannamei (whiteleg shrimp), Penaeus monodon (giant tiger prawn) and Penaeus stylirostris (blue shrimp). It added that the measure is aimed at strengthening national food safety controls, particularly to ensure that seabass and shrimp entering the local market comply with established safety and quality standards.

items.title: [UPDATED] Samsuri replaces Hamzah as new Opposition Leader
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Perikatan Nasional (PN) chairman and Terengganu Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Moktar has been appointed as the new opposition leader. PN secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan said the decision to appoint Samsuri was made during a PN Supreme Council meeting here today. Chaired by Samsuri, the meeting was attended by the PN supreme council members.

items.title: Malaysia scam losses surge from RM1.28b in 2023 to RM2.77b in 2025, investment fraud leads cases, says deputy minister
items.summary: KULAI, May 16 — The Communications Ministry, through the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), is stepping up its Safe Internet Campaign at all levels of the community following a sharp rise in online scam cases, which recorded losses of RM2.77 billion last year, said Deputy Minister Teo Nie Ching. On the campaign, Teo, who is also Kulai MP, said the ministry adopts different approaches for different target groups to ensure digital awareness messages are effectively delivered, with cyberbullying emphasised at primary school level. As of April 30, she said the campaign has been carried out in 10,134 schools, comprising national schools, vernacular schools, secondary schools, as well as teacher training institutes and higher education institutions, reaching nearly 900,000 participants.

items.title: Picnic turns tragic as man drowns, brother goes missing
items.summary: KOTA BELUD: A family picnic turned tragic when a 23-year-old man drowned while his younger brother remains missing this morning. Kota Belud police chief Superintendent Shahrudin Mat Husain said Zul Hakim was found dead by villagers from Kampung Melangkap before the matter was reported to the Fire and Rescue Department. Meanwhile, Kota Belud Fire and Rescue Station chief Elgie Bakuk said the department received an emergency call via the MERS999 line at 9.26am.

items.title: Federal Territories PKR Youth chief quits party
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Federal Territories PKR Youth chief has quit the party, citing the party's drift from the reform principles he had believed in since his days as an activist. He announced his resignation from all party positions effective immediately. Asheeq said that his departure from the party would not affect his commitment to the Batu community.

items.title: Deputy minister: 6,680 issued notices for littering nationwide since January, teens and foreigners among offenders
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 16 — A total of 6,680 individuals nationwide, including 15 teenagers, have been issued notices of offence (NPK) for littering in public places since January. Deputy Housing and Local Government Minister Aiman Athirah Sabu said the figure includes 1,866 foreigners, among them individuals from Indonesia, Singapore, China and Bangladesh. Also present was the Solid Waste and Public Cleansing Management Corporation (SWCorp) chief executive officer Khalid Mohamed.

items.title: MEF reaffirms commitment to workplace mental health protection
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Employers Federation (MEF) has reaffirmed its commitment to supporting national efforts to strengthen workplace mental health protection and psychosocial risk management. President Datuk Syed Hussain Syed Husman said the world of work is changing rapidly, and employers are increasingly confronted with psychosocial challenges arising from excessive workloads, burnout, workplace harassment, emotional strain, and blurred work-life boundaries. "Employers, especially MSMEs, require adequate time, practical guidance, awareness, and capacity-building support to progressively adopt psychosocial risk management measures effectively.

items.title: Penang pushes active water-saving culture, aims to cut domestic use to 250 litres per person daily
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 16 — Perbadanan Bekalan Air Pulau Pinang (PBAPP) aims to reduce the state’s domestic water consumption to 250 litres per capita per day (LPCD) this year through various water-saving initiatives involving multiple stakeholders. Its chief executive officer, Datuk K. Pathmanathan, said the target is achievable if domestic users consistently practise water conservation, as even small savings at the household level can have a significant impact on overall water usage in the state. “In 2022, Penang’s LPCD rate stood at 307 litres per day, while in the following year it rose slightly to 308 litres per day.

items.title: Man charged with death threat to woman in Kota Kinabalu court
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 16 — A 21-year-old man was charged in the Magistrate’s Court here with threatening to cause death to a woman. Meanwhile, a 41-year-old man was fined RM2,800 or six months’ jail for keeping a stolen wall fan. Maarof Garib pleaded guilty to having the stolen wall fan belonging to Shell Station Wisma Innoprise, Jalan UMS, at the male surau of the petrol station at about 12.30pm on May 9.

items.title: Anwar to teachers: Stay positive in face of criticism
items.summary: He said teachers must continue to play a central role in shaping students' character and values, despite being increasingly affected by social issues that spill into schools. "Teachers appear to be burdened. Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek, Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad, and Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail.

items.title: Subsidy reforms not strictly reliant on income classification, says Economy Minister
items.summary: Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the ministry avoids rigid classifications such as "T20", as household income alone does not fully reflect economic reality. Akmal said subsidy rationalisation remains part of the government's wider economic restructuring agenda aimed at ensuring assistance is delivered more effectively to the intended groups. Akmal said discussions on more targeted subsidy distribution are ongoing, particularly to ensure government programmes reach their intended beneficiaries.

items.title: Driver loses control, crashes car into Kuching coffee shop, two sent to hospital
items.summary: KUCHING, May 16 — An elderly man lost control of his sedan and crashed into a coffee shop near Jalan Rock traffic light junction here today. See Hua Daily News reported that the incident occurred at around 11am, and the elderly driver was unharmed. Personnel from the Civil Defence Force responded to the incident and arrived at the scene to provide assistance It was also learnt that two individuals from the coffeeshop were sent to hospital by ambulance, possibly due to shock from the incident.

items.title: Honouring teachers as the pulse of the nation's future
items.summary: This year's theme is more than a mere symbolic expression - it represents a profound recognition of teachers as the driving force behind national development and societal progress. The Malaysia Education Blueprint (RPM) 2026–2035, launched by the MOE in January 2026, positions teachers as the primary drivers of national education transformation. Teachers' wellbeing remains a national priority.

items.title: PKR sec-gen says Pasir Gudang MP does not speak for govt in resignation call over UEC varsity decision
items.summary: Yesterday, Hassan called on Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek and Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Zambry Abd Kadir to resign, claiming they had failed to defend the national education policy involving public universities. The Higher Education Ministry had earlier announced that graduates from tahfiz schools, Chinese independent secondary schools and other institutions outside the national education system would be allowed to pursue selected programmes at public higher education institutions, subject to conditions. The move followed a Cabinet decision on admission pathways for students outside the national education system into public higher education institutions.

items.title: Anwar: Educators must accept criticism positively and show resilience in facing challenges
items.summary: IPOH, May 16 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said educators need to be big-hearted in accepting challenges and criticism positively, especially regarding shortcomings and problems involving students. He said education cannot be separated from being linked to personal, character, human or humanity and any problems that arise are often linked to schools until they sometimes burden educators. “Social problems certainly affect children in schools and certainly cause commotion.

items.title: Overnight queues, RM50 ‘runners’ and shattered hopes at KL Audemars Piguet x Swatch launch (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — By the time the sun rose over Mid Valley Megamall this morning, Ayu Omar and her daughter Allesya had already spent nearly 10 hours waiting for a watch from the new Audemars Piguet x Swatch “Royal Pop” collection. Overnight queue for limited drop “Firstly, I like Swatch. At around 9.15am, a Swatch staff member informed the crowd that only a limited number of watches were available.

items.title: Selangor launches Bantuan Rahmah Insan Istimewa to expand support for PwDs, says state exco
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 16 — The Selangor government today launched the Bantuan Rahmah Insan Istimewa Selangor (BRAIS) 2026 programme to expand existing support for the welfare of persons with disabilities (PwDs). State Women Development and Social Welfare Committee chairman Anfaal Saari said the initiative, under the Yayasan Anak Istimewa Selangor (YANIS), is a rebranding of the Bantuan Khas Anak Istimewa Selangor (ANIS) aimed at delivering more comprehensive and high-impact development for PwDs in the state. Elaborating further, Anfaal said that in addition to the new categories, BRAIS 2026 will retain existing support, including assistance for therapy centre fees (RM1,200), special equipment (up to RM3,000) and special education school fees (RM120).

items.title: Education Ministry unveils 12 initiatives to empower teachers at national Teachers’ Day celebration
items.summary: Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the Google Certified Educator (GCE) Level 1 and Level 2 certification courses would continue to be offered free of charge to all teachers to support their professional development in digital literacy. She said the initiative would benefit teachers nationwide, with the ministry bearing the cost of RM40 per teacher for GCE Level 1 and RM100 per teacher for GCE Level 2. Fadhlina also said the ministry would offer a special Federal Training Award to teachers serving in schools in hardship areas including hospital classes, floating schools and rural schools in P1, P2 and P3 areas.

items.title: On Teachers’ Day, Anwar says educators crucial in shaping values amid complex global challenges
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the role of teachers is becoming ever more crucial in an increasingly complex world, particularly in shaping a generation with strong moral character and inner strength grounded in human values. He said teachers’ responsibilities are no longer confined to imparting knowledge, but also include building confidence, upholding human dignity, and instilling hope and aspiration in life. Anwar said that without values and ethics, development would only result in progress without direction.

items.title: Safety concerns near Empire Residence prompt halt to 26-storey condo project in Damansara Perdana
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 16 — Concerns over a hillslope condominium project behind Empire Residence in Damansara Perdana have prompted the Petaling Jaya City Council (MBPJ) to issue a stop-work order for the next two weeks. Damansara MP Gobind Singh Deo said the directive was issued following complaints from residents over mud and water flow during heavy rain, which they believe is originating from the construction site, The Star reported today. He said Class 3 slopes may be developed under strict conditions while Class 4 slopes are generally not allowed for permanent structures.

items.title: Abang Johari: Sarawak eyes 700 million Asean market with new Tanjung Embang Port
items.summary: KUCHING, May 16 — Sarawak is targeting a market of 700 million Asean residents through the development of the modern Tanjung Embang Port and strategic logistics infrastructure to strengthen the state’s position as a regional trade hub, said Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Openg. Officiating at the Kuching Port Authority (KPA) 65th Anniversary Celebration Dinner here last night, the Premier said that geographically, Sarawak is located at the heart of Asean. Abang Johari, who is also the Minister of Finance and New Economy, shared that operations at Senari Port will eventually relocate to Tanjung Embang Port, given that the former may eventually no longer be able to accommodate the increase in cargo resulting from Sarawak’s economic growth.

items.title: KK court orders man to enter defence in sexual assault case involving 17‑year‑old girl
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 16 — The Sessions Court here has ordered a man to enter his defence on a charge of committing physical sexual assault against a teenage girl. Judge Amir Shah Amir Hassan made the order on the 63-year-old after ruling that the prosecution had established a prima facie case against him. Deputy public prosecutor Bryan Francis handled the prosecution while the accused was represented by counsel Mohd Al Hafidz Mohd Lokman.

items.title: Sepanggar body in bin case: Court grants extra month for suspect’s psychiatric report
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 16 — The Magistrates’ Court here has allowed a one-month extension for the mental evaluation of a fishmonger charged with murdering a woman, whose body parts were found at a communal rubbish bin in Indah Permai Sepanggar last February. Magistrate Dzul Elmy Yunus granted the application for Piluta Samad, 71, after the prosecution informed the court that his psychiatric report was not ready. Piluta’s legal team led by Datuk Ram Singh raised no objection to the extension request.

items.title: Kelantan police remand married couple as probe continues into Bachok murder of disabled man
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 16 — A married couple has been remanded for seven and five days respectively to assist in the investigation into the murder of a disabled man at Ladang Merdeka Cherbu, Padang Lati, Bachok, in April. The 26-year-old male suspect has been remanded for seven days until next Friday, while his 27-year-old wife has been remanded for five days until Wednesday. The remand order was issued by Magistrate Fakhrul Razzi Abd Hamid at the Magistrate’s Court here to facilitate investigations under Section 302 of the Penal Code.

items.title: ‘Sandwich generation’ relief: EPF i-Legasi enables retirement savings transfers to family
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — The Employees Provident Fund’s (EPF) newly introduced i-Legasi facility could provide much-needed financial planning support for Malaysia’s ‘sandwich generation’, who often juggle the responsibilities of caring for ageing parents while raising children. Amid rising living costs and longer life expectancy, the facility allows eligible members to transfer part of their retirement savings to immediate family members, such as spouses or children, to support long-term family financial planning. Nik Maheran said initiatives such as i-Legasi could encourage earlier discussions on retirement and family financial planning, adding that intergenerational support remains a deeply rooted aspect of Asian family values.

items.title: Sabah raid uncovers wildlife hoard of rhino horns, pangolin scales, elephant bones in former ranger’s Tuaran home
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — A man believed to be a former forest ranger was arrested after allegedly possessing various parts of protected wildlife, estimated to be worth RM589,230, in a raid in Tuaran, Sabah, yesterday. Bukit Aman Internal Security and Public Order Department’s Wildlife Crime Bureau/Special Intelligence Investigation (WCB/PSK) commander Datuk Mohd Zaki Ashar Datuk Mohd Zaki Ashar said the 61-year-old man was arrested around 7.15 am in an operation resulting from the collaboration between WCB/PSK and the Sabah Wildlife Department. He said the case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Sabah Wildlife Conservation Enactment 1997 for possessing or hunting protected wildlife, as well as Section 41(2) of the same enactment for possessing or selling protected wildlife.

items.title: Taiping pair convicted as teens for killing girl’s brother remain under indefinite detention after court rejects bail bid
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 16 — The Court of Appeal today dismissed applications by two former students, a boy and a girl, seeking a stay of execution and bail pending the hearing of their appeal. They had appealed against a High Court decision that found them guilty of murder and ordered them to be detained at the pleasure of the Sultan of Perak. Justice Datuk Ahmad Kamal Md Shahid, who chaired a three-member bench, ruled that the reasons advanced by both applicants did not constitute special or exceptional circumstances to justify granting bail.

items.title: MetMalaysia warns of short-term thunderstorms with intense rainfall in Perlis, Kedah, Penang and parts of Sabah, Johor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit all northern states in Peninsular Malaysia as well as several areas in Pahang, Johor, Sabah and Labuan until noon today, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). In a weather warning issued at 9am, MetMalaysia said the conditions are forecast to affect the whole of Perlis, Kedah and Penang, as well as Labuan. In Perak, the affected areas are expected to include Kerian, Larut, Matang dan Selama, Hulu Perak and Kuala Kangsar.
